content: read reviews/118.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/118.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/118.md.

    18→- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Erdős–Hajnal conjecture on partition ordinals).
    19→- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er87], [Er90], [Er95d], [Er97f], [Sc99], [Sc10], [Da99], [La00], [HST10].
    20→
    21→## 4. Namespace
    22→
    23→- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos118 ... end Erdos118` (lines 53, 99).
    24→- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos118`).
    25→- [x] `open Ordinal` appears **before** the namespace block (line 51).
    26→
    27→## 5. Custom Definitions
    28→
    29→- [x] `OrdinalSet` (abbrev, line 57) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 55–56).
    30→- [x] `OrdPartition` (def, line 68) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 59–67).
    31→- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
    32→- [x] Types use `UpperCamelCase`: `OrdinalSet`, `OrdPartition`.
    33→- [x] `noncomputable` not needed — `OrdinalSet` is a subtype alias and `OrdPartition` returns `Prop`.
    34→- [x] No `local notation` used (none needed for this problem).
    35→- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. *(Optional — not required.)*
    36→
    37→## 6. Theorem Statements
    38→
    39→- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 95).
    40→- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_118` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    41→- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 3 5]` (line 94).
    42→- [x] Has a docstring (lines 75–93).
    43→- [x] Docstring references source papers: [Er87], [Er90], [Er95d], [Er97f], [Sc99], [Sc10], [Da99], [La00], [HST10].
    44→
    45→### `answer()` usage
    46→
    47→The original problem asks a yes/no question: whether every partition ordinal α satisfying α → (α, 3)² must also satisfy α → (α, n)² for all n ≥ 3. This is **Case 1** (yes/no question).
    48→
    49→- [x] **Case 1 applies.** The problem asks "must ... hold?", confirmed by the source.
    50→- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P` — correct since the conjecture was **disproved**.
    51→- [x] `answer(False)` means "no" to the original question, which is correct (the conjecture is false).
    52→- [x] RHS `∀ (α : Ordinal), OrdPartition α α 3 → ∀ n : ℕ, 3 ≤ n → OrdPartition α α (↑n)` correctly formalizes the question as originally asked.
    53→- [x] Quantifiers are **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.
    54→
    55→## 7. Category Attribute
    56→
    57→- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
    58→- [x] Value is `research solved` — appropriate since the conjecture was disproved by Schipperus and Darby but the proof is not formally verified in Lean.
    59→
    60→## 8. AMS Attribute
    61→
    62→- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
    63→- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 3, 5).
    64→- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 3, 5.
    65→- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    66→- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes: 3 (Mathematical logic and foundations), 5 (Combinatorics). Both appropriate for an ordinal partition relation problem.
    67→
    68→## 9. Proof Bodies
    69→
    70→- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
    71→- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
    72→- [x] N/A: file is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
    73→- [x] No informal proof sketches included for the theorem (the docstring discusses the disproof context appropriately without sketching the proof).
    74→- [x] No `native_decide`.
    75→
    76→## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    77→
    78→- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single conjecture statement.
    79→
    80→## 11. Code Quality
    81→
    82→- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
    83→- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `↔`, `∀`, `≤`, `ℕ`, `↑`, `<`, `→`.
    84→- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    85→- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    86→- [x] Lines are reasonably short (well within 100 characters).
    87→- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    88→
    89→## 12. Build Verification
    90→
    91→- [ ] Not verified in this review. No obvious issues; `sorry` warning expected for the proof body.
    92→
    93→---
    94→
    95→## Summary
    96→
    97→**PASS.** The file is fully conformant with the checklist. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace structure, custom definitions, theorem statement, attributes, and code quality all meet requirements. The `answer(False) ↔ P` formulation correctly captures Case 1 (yes/no question answered negatively), the `OrdPartition` definition faithfully encodes the standard ordinal partition relation α → (β, γ)², and the category `research solved` is appropriate for a disproved conjecture without formal verification.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/118.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` on line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–49).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 118`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/118`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (Erdős–Hajnal conjecture on partition ordinals).
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [Er87], [Er90], [Er95d], [Er97f], [Sc99], [Sc10], [Da99], [La00], [HST10].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos118 ... end Erdos118` (lines 53, 99).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos118`).
- [x] `open Ordinal` appears **before** the namespace block (line 51).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `OrdinalSet` (abbrev, line 57) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 55–56).
- [x] `OrdPartition` (def, line 68) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 59–67).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] Types use `UpperCamelCase`: `OrdinalSet`, `OrdPartition`.
- [x] `noncomputable` not needed — `OrdinalSet` is a subtype alias and `OrdPartition` returns `Prop`.
- [x] No `local notation` used (none needed for this problem).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. *(Optional — not required.)*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 95).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_118` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 3 5]` (line 94).
- [x] Has a docstring (lines 75–93).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: [Er87], [Er90], [Er95d], [Er97f], [Sc99], [Sc10], [Da99], [La00], [HST10].

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks a yes/no question: whether every partition ordinal α satisfying α → (α, 3)² must also satisfy α → (α, n)² for all n ≥ 3. This is **Case 1** (yes/no question).

- [x] **Case 1 applies.** The problem asks "must ... hold?", confirmed by the source.
- [x] Theorem type is `answer(False) ↔ P` — correct since the conjecture was **disproved**.
- [x] `answer(False)` means "no" to the original question, which is correct (the conjecture is false).
- [x] RHS `∀ (α : Ordinal), OrdPartition α α 3 → ∀ n : ℕ, 3 ≤ n → OrdPartition α α (↑n)` correctly formalizes the question as originally asked.
- [x] Quantifiers are **after** `answer(False)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` — appropriate since the conjecture was disproved by Schipperus and Darby but the proof is not formally verified in Lean.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (two: 3, 5).
- [x] Numbers in **ascending numeric order**: 3, 5.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes: 3 (Mathematical logic and foundations), 5 (Combinatorics). Both appropriate for an ordinal partition relation problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] N/A: file is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`.
- [x] No informal proof sketches included for the theorem (the docstring discusses the disproof context appropriately without sketching the proof).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A. No variants present. The problem is a single conjecture statement.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `↔`, `∀`, `≤`, `ℕ`, `↑`, `<`, `→`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (well within 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified in this review. No obvious issues; `sorry` warning expected for the proof body.

---

## Summary

**PASS.** The file is fully conformant with the checklist. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace structure, custom definitions, theorem statement, attributes, and code quality all meet requirements. The `answer(False) ↔ P` formulation correctly captures Case 1 (yes/no question answered negatively), the `OrdPartition` definition faithfully encodes the standard ordinal partition relation α → (β, γ)², and the category `research solved` is appropriate for a disproved conjecture without formal verification.

content.text: The review summary says **PASS**. No changes needed.
